channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-16822] test IPv6 NIDs in sanity-lnet.sh
item.description:
It should be possible to add test cases to sanity-lnet.sh that are using IPv6 addresses that are (probably) already being assigned to the test nodes in autotest.

As a starting point, a patch that is adding a new test_0 that is just printing out the interfaces configured in the VM node with ifconfig (or new-fangled alternative) would give us useful information and not be harmful in the future.

At that point, if the client and MDS/OSS nodes have IPv6 addresses assigned, it should be possible to add subtests that are configuring IPv6 NIDs:

  • use lnetctl to add IPv6 NIDs to a node
  • use lnetctl ping to ping the local and peer NIDs with IPv4 and IPv6 addresses to see all NIDs reported
  • configure and run lnet selftest with the IPv6 NIDs
  • etc.
  • unconfigure IPv6 NIDs from the nodes

I suspect that this can be done without any changes to autotest, since sanity-lnet.sh is unmounting Lustre anyway, and there should already be enough IPv6 infrastructure on master to test at the LNet level.

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.

comments.comment: All patches tracked under this ticket seem to be merged for 2.16.

"Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>" merged in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/55463/
Subject: LU-16822 tests: Modify local_node to check for IPv6
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set:
Commit: 1bbb066512ba6bc39253e2f7d3a020634d7e9f2a

"Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>" merged in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/55422/
Subject: LU-16822 tests: Force IPv6 testing in mixed environment
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set:
Commit: c8ca47daac7af4268a0ea698f962e441f8a69108

I commented in LU-17889, but maybe this ticket is a better place since that ticket has now been closed.

I've asked that a more clear error message is added to mount.lustre and mkfs.lustre when a link-local fe80: address is used, but something should likely be added into lnetctl or similar to print a very clear message like "Unsupported link-local IPv6 NID type 'fe80:...'" instead of just returning "-22" in that case, so this can be caught very early during configuration instead of later on when these addresses are being used.

"Chris Horn <chris.horn@hpe.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/55465
Subject: LU-16822 tests: Cleanup fake interface usage
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 4e6ab03a332c088e58d77afe715475dc824930f1

This patch will be pushed under https://jira.whamcloud.com/browse/LU-17989

"Chris Horn <chris.horn@hpe.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/55463
Subject: LU-16822 tests: Modify local_node to check for IPv6
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 5ebd693f91f079b244c5802d3389c0c011d3d67b

"James Simmons <jsimmons@infradead.org>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/55422
Subject: LU-16822: test IPv6 NIDs in sanity-lnet.sh
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: a1c38867ec4ab652d6bfe292265e93ffdb72ac85

Status update:

A hot fix to enable proper testing has landed. Working with Srikanth for the final patch for this ticket so we can use a environmentally variable to test IPv6. The good news is many patches to fix various test issues and increase the testing coverage are ready for the next master-next batch. Peter any status on Maloo IPv6 setup ?
One more patch to landed to enable full testing.
Merged for 2.16

"Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>" merged in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/55129/
Subject: LU-16822 lnet: use proper Netlink flags for setup
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set:
Commit: 9d9b8cbaa4123df708a072acd1a4f30120075418

"Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>" merged in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/53599/
Subject: LU-16822 tests: Setup IPv6 with fake network device
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set:
Commit: b0ab0c9fa54a9b28db677133efd219d6720fec15

"Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>" merged in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/53728/
Subject: LU-16822 tests: Update sanity-lnet router tests for IPv6
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set:
Commit: 674bd8beb7379651f25179d026cd547e0f58b81e

Pushing the testing patch to maloo I noticed that IPv6 is not coming up. The reason for that is it appears Maloo nodes have only setup local link IPv6 addresses. Those are only visible to the local node. We need full fledge IPv6 addresses. Something like:

 inet6 2601:8c1:c100:4ac0::de0c/128 scope global dynamic noprefixroute  
      valid_lft 341738sec preferred_lft 341738sec

"James Simmons <jsimmons@infradead.org>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/55129
Subject: LU-16822 lnet: setup IPv6 LNet by default to test
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 4313fef0621e3a900eda409f1260a4b097327c27

"Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>" merged in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/51818/
Subject: LU-16822 lnet: always initialize IPv6 at start up
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set:
Commit: ab6c8bd18e1441f803a6b090a555eb30b1c527b7

Their will be a special flag to pass to lctl net up / lnetctl lnet configure. We can set it with a bash variable. Be aware it will be some time before we can do testing. Right now several non IPv6 LNet patches are colliding with the IPv6 work. Its a mess right now.
simmonsja That should work, all test nodes are provisioned with an IPV6 address. If your patch uses IPV6 by default, is there a way to configure the cluster to use IPV4?

Hi Minh. Are the test nodes ready? Once patch https://review.whamcloud.com/#/c/fs/lustre-release/+/54412 landed all the basic functionality can be tested with IPv6. I have a local patch that allows using IPv6 addresses by default on startup but I haven't pushed it yet. Once the next batch of patches from master-next land I will push and we can try testing on the maloo nodes if they are ready.
hornc do you have any update?

hornc, I don't think we can remove the IPv4 interfaces from the test nodes completely, so for the time being it means that sanity-lnet can add tests that are "adding" IPv6 interfaces explicitly, but I don't think we can have it run exclusively IPv6 testing today.

colmstea I'm not sure what a "config AT" is, but the easiest way to get auster to use IPv6 is to remove the IPv4 address(es) from the network interface that gets used with LNet. If the LNet interface only has an IPv6 address, then that will get used. If it has both IPv4 and IPv6 then the IPv4 address is used.

There are patches under development that allow specifying whether to us v4 or v6 when both are present, but those patches are not ready/landed yet.
mkvardakov merged a change that sets up IPV6 addresses on all test nodes (https://review.whamcloud.com/c/private/lab/+/54024/). Is there a config AT sets to signal to Auster to use IPV6?

"Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>" merged in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/53727/
Subject: LU-16822 tests: Skip tests lacking large NID support
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set:
Commit: 9e0bf3cc5b92c73229b68125b39451b211a57a1f

James, I'd assume that this "IPv6 first" choice would be a socklnd module option? Otherwise, if this is a build-time change it would introduce compatibility issues for systems being updated when they would not actually need to use IPv6.

For IPv6 testing with sanity-lnet I created two approaches. One is with patch https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/53605 which allows setting up an NI using a specific IP. The second is reworking lnet_inet_enumerate() to register IPv6 first if available. Then lctl net up with use IPv6 first if available. Both approaches can work but we should discuss which approach to use.
Not the final patch. Just a step forward to make IPv6 testing possible.

"James Simmons <jsimmons@infradead.org>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/53599
Subject: LU-16822 tests: Setup IPv6 with fake network device
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: b3bc7d66a7f25280fb90be7e9b2398a3c867c25a
With my Ubuntu systems I have IPv6 setup so I tried  MGSNID with IPv6 to see how far it would go. Sadly we are using lctl net up which defaults to IPv4. I have to think about a solution.

"James Simmons <jsimmons@infradead.org>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/51818
Subject: LU-16822 test: collect IP setup
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 819957820eae7d884e04d393c65e6391a947bf4d
Chris, since you wrote most of sanity-lnet.sh could you please chime in with suggestions on how this could best be done.
